title: Split Foam graph into three workspaces (backbone / personal / full-PRIVATE)
current_state: Single Foam workspace indexes vault/**/*.md, which mixes (a) stable architectural notes â€” tickets, sprints, retros, per-ticket briefs, PI.md, ADRs, CHECKPOINTS â€” with (b) personal/relations content that grows fast and is already in .git/info/exclude â€” vault/notes/memory/** (fact files, entity hubs, dedup output), vault/notes/sessions/** (normie chat archives), vault/notes/north_star.md. Foam Show Graph renders everything in one canvas. As entity hubs and memory facts accumulate, the backbone gets buried in personal nodes. Also a screen-share / screenshot privacy risk â€” the architectural graph is shareable, the personal one is not.
structural_problem: Conflating 'shareable engineering spine' with 'private personal data' in the same graph. Two different audiences (Ash navigating architecture vs Ash reviewing personal memory) share one lens. As the personal subtree grows (T-096 entity hubs, ongoing dedup), the architectural signal-to-noise drops.
target_state: Three VS Code workspace files at repo root, each with their own foam.workspace.includeGlobs / excludeGlobs: (1) pi-backbone.code-workspace â€” daily driver, shareable; indexes PI.md, README.md, docs/**/*.md, CHECKPOINTS/**, vault/notes/{status,tickets,per-ticket,sprints,retros,templates}/**. (2) pi-personal.code-workspace â€” entity recall, never shared; indexes only vault/notes/{memory,sessions}/** + north_star.md. (3) pi-full-PRIVATE.code-workspace â€” opt-in union for occasional cross-graph queries; indexes everything but exclude/data/logs/_archive/_private. docs/vscode-setup.md documents the three and which to open by default.
files_affected: .gitignore (already ignores pi-personal + pi-full-PRIVATE via *.code-workspace blanket? â€” verify; if not, add to .git/info/exclude)
success_criteria: Three .code-workspace files at repo root with distinct include/exclude globs
Opening pi-backbone, running Foam: Show Graph â€” zero nodes from vault/notes/memory/**, vault/notes/sessions/**, north_star.md
Opening pi-personal â€” zero nodes from PI.md, docs/, CHECKPOINTS/, tickets/
Opening pi-full-PRIVATE â€” shows everything, including cross-graph edges if any exist
docs/vscode-setup.md has a 'Three Graphs' section explaining which workspace to open when
pi-personal + pi-full-PRIVATE filenames either gitignored or in .git/info/exclude so they don't leak personal includeGlobs hints publicly
pi-backbone.code-workspace IS committable (architectural â€” describes shareable structure)
risk_notes: Low risk â€” pure IDE config, no code paths touched, no vault content edited. Worst case: an include/exclude glob is off and one workspace renders extra nodes. Reversible by editing the JSON.
migration_plan: 1. Write three .code-workspace files at repo root
2. Verify globs by opening each in VS Code and running Foam: Show Graph
3. Add pi-personal.code-workspace + pi-full-PRIVATE.code-workspace to .git/info/exclude (local-only; only pi-backbone gets committed)
4. Extend docs/vscode-setup.md with Three Graphs section + table
5. Don't change default open behavior â€” Ash opens whichever workspace he wants when he wants
resolution: Already fully implemented. Three .code-workspace files (pi-backbone, pi-personal, pi-full-PRIVATE) exist at repo root with correct foam.files.include/exclude globs. pi-personal and pi-full-PRIVATE are in .git/info/exclude. docs/vscode-setup.md has complete Three Graphs section with table and recommendations. verify.py PASS.
